title: stachys beckeana
intro: Stachys beckeana, also known as Wood Betony, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family, native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its hairy leaves and purple flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of iridoid glycosides and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Stachys beckeana include relief from headaches, fever, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce stress and anxiety. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
